businessModel.text: Revenue from Wealth Management (advisory and brokerage fees, ~45%), Investment Banking (M&A advisory, ECM, debt underwriting, ~25%), and Institutional Securities (sales & trading, equity research, ~30%).

recentCatalysts.text: Wealth Management net new assets +$200bn+ per year; fee-based revenue now 50% of div WM income E*Trade integration completed Q2 FY25 — $3.2tn in client assets Investment Banking revenue surged +26% YoY Q1 2025 on M&A and IPO pipeline MTU (Medium-term note) programme expanding into Asia retail structured products
items: Wealth Management net new assets +$200bn+ per year; fee-based revenue now 50% of div WM income
E*Trade integration completed Q2 FY25 — $3.2tn in client assets
Investment Banking revenue surged +26% YoY Q1 2025 on M&A and IPO pipeline
MTU (Medium-term note) programme expanding into Asia retail structured products

keyRisks.text: 1. Balance-sheet sensitive to Q3/Q4 2026 rate volatility if Fed resumes hiking 2. Wealth-management front-book revenue depends on equity market levels 3. E*Trade retail client base is lower-margin and rate-sensitive (cash drag) 4. Credit quality in investment bank's leveraged loan book — potential deterioration in stress scenario 5. Technology spend: platform rebuild costing $800M incremental annually through FY26

risks.risk: 1. Balance-sheet sensitive to Q3/Q4 2026 rate volatility if Fed resumes hiking
2. Wealth-management front-book revenue depends on equity market levels
3. E*Trade retail client base is lower-margin and rate-sensitive (cash drag)
4. Credit quality in investment bank's leveraged loan book — potential deterioration in stress scenario
5. Technology spend: platform rebuild costing $800M incremental annually through FY26

whoShouldOwn.text: Wealth-management secular growth thesis combined with investment-banking cyclical upside. Hybrid bank-technology positioning. NOT suitable for pure rate-squeeze plays or investors needing margin expansion clarity in near term.

entryExit.text: Accumulate at $160–$172 (10–15% from 52-week low). HOLD $172–$190. REDUCE above $190. Track: wealth management net new assets and investment banking fee trends. | Accumulate at $160–$172 (10–15% from 52-week low). | REDUCE above $190.

sources.marketData: Live quote data sourced via DYOR HQ data pipeline (Google Sheets + Yahoo Finance). Fields include price, market capitalisation, 52-week range, P/E, EPS, and volume.
sources.companyFilings: Public company filings, regulatory announcements, investor presentations, and RNS where referenced in the analysis.
